title: jasminum arborescens
intro: Jasminum arborescens, also known as the winter jasmine, is a deciduous shrub native to China, characterized by its evergreen leaves and fragrant, white flowers that bloom from winter to early spring. The medicinal properties of Jasminum arborescens include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its use in traditional medicine. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce stress, and alleviate digestive issues, as well as its ability to boost the immune system. Jasminum arborescens is traditionally prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety.
